feedback.text: Hello As a game developer I think it is very important to uphold consumer rights in the space of video games. We create these great stories and experiences not only for certain individuals, we want them to stay for years to come. We create them for everyone to enjoy and enable those who want to purchase a copy and enjoy it for themselves, to own it and pass it on. In a sense, video games should be held up to the same standards as books, everyone who owns a copy should be able to do with it as they please, because they purchased it. Imagine a world where we would have to give up a book just because the author died or decided the contents are not good anymore or another edition is coming up. Video games sales should be more fair and marketed more fairly. I wholeheartedly support this initiative, as it raises important issues and puts them in the public eye. Thank you.
